---
title: "Statistical DUS Overexpression analysis"
author: "Oliwia Hillestad Waz"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(Biostrings)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats) # Useful for reordering the species axis
library(scales) # Required for the rescale function

```


Based on manual overexpression calculation done in excel, a heat-map is constructed:

```{r}
# Load data from excel
enrichment_df <- read_excel("C:/Users/oliwi/OneDrive - OsloMet/00. Master Thesies/Bioinformatics/Enrichment_calculation.xlsx")

# Wide excel data into long format
df_long <- enrichment_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("DUS"), 
    names_to = "Motif", 
    values_to = "Enrichment_Ratio"
  )

# Order x-axis from 10 to 4 (left to right)
df_long$Motif <- factor(df_long$Motif, 
                        levels = c("DUS10 ratio", "DUS9 ratio", "DUS8 ratio", "DUS7 ratio", "DUS6 ratio", "DUS5 ratio", "DUS4 ratio"))

# Construct Heat-map 
ggplot(df_long, 
       aes(x = Motif, 
           y = fct_reorder(Species, Enrichment_Ratio, .fun = max), 
           fill = Enrichment_Ratio)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") + 
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Enrichment_Ratio, 1)), size = 3, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#f7fcf5", "#f7fcf5", "#CC9933", "#CC9933", "#CC9900"), # Light to dark green
    trans = "log10",
    name = "Enrichment\nRatio (log10)",
    breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000),
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "ChromoDUS Enrichment Across Species",
    subtitle = "Values represent Observed/Expected ratios based on GC-bias",
    x = "Motif",
    y = "Species") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
